description: Cloud at or near the ground is 'nuphari' when it noticeably limits sight through the air. The boundary with 'wheiru' (mist) follows practical visibility rather than one universal distance. Fog can conceal a route or landmark without making either one absent.
articulatory_notes: Rest the tongue tip against the backs of the upper teeth for voiced dental 'n' and let nasal voice enter high-back rounded 'u'. Bring the lips close together for unvoiced bilabial 'ph' and release the narrow breath into stressed central 'a'. Tap or briefly trill the tongue at the alveolar ridge, then finish with high-front unrounded 'i'.

pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Fog changes how paths and shorelines can be followed. Bells, lights, and familiar distances then take on more of the journey. Travellers and boat crews learned local fog through its seasons, winds, and sounds rather than through sight alone.

semantic_domains.nature: ground-level suspended droplets within local weather
semantic_domains.physical: airborne water dense enough to reduce visibility
